April: Cherry blossoms in Parc La Grange, lake-fish lunches in Hermance, and Easter chocolate at Du Rhône.
Watches & Wonders buzz, Old Town strolls, and golden Mont Blanc evenings from Pont du Mont-Blanc.
August: Quiet boulevards on August 1 (Swiss National Day), harbour fireworks, and Alpine day-trips from Cornavin.
Lake swims at sunset, Carouge gelato runs, and family-quiet Geneva while locals decamp to their chalets.
December: Escalade festival's torchlit procession, Noël au Quai market by the lake, and chocolate-pot marmite tradition.
Christmas lights on Rue du Rhône, fondue in the Old Town, and a 90-minute train straight to Megève.
February: Sunlit ski Saturdays in Verbier, candlelit raclette in the Old Town, and harbour walks under bise winds.
Patek Philippe museum mornings, jazz at Chat Noir, and warm bistros tucked into Plainpalais.
January: Snow-dusted Old Town, fondue suppers in Carouge, and a 90-minute train to Chamonix powder.
Steaming hot chocolate at Auer, sauna at Bains des Pâquis, and museum afternoons by the Rhône.
March: First crocus at the Jardin Anglais, terraces tentatively reopening, and spring ski days in the Portes du Soleil.
Salève cable-car panoramas, Mandement vineyard prunings, and longer light along Quai Wilson.
May: Lake swimming reopens, harbour Mouettes hum every 10 minutes, and Carouge terraces fill at twilight.
Marché de Carouge mornings, Salève hikes in wildflower bloom, and Léman Express trips to Évian and Annecy.
June: Long golden evenings on the quais, apéro at Bains des Pâquis, and Lake Geneva at swim-able 22°C.
Open-air concerts of Musiques en été, sunset Mouettes crossings, and bikes flying along the Voie Verte.
July: Fêtes de Genève fireworks, lakefront DJs, and lazy plage afternoons at Genève-Plage.
Mont Blanc panoramas from La Dôle, late-night Pâquis dining, and Mouettes water-buses every 10 minutes.

reasons_to_visit.reason: Best Weather Window of the Year
reasons_to_visit.details: Warm sunny days in the low 20s, cool nights, very low rainfall, and clear visibility for Mont Blanc — many Geneva regulars consider September the city's perfect month.
Mandement, Satigny, and Dardagny vineyards are at full harvest, with wine festivals and open cellars across the Geneva canton.
Grand Théâtre, Victoria Hall, BFM, and museums launch new programmes after the summer break — a strong month for opera, classical music, and major exhibitions.
Salève, Jura crest trails, and Mont Blanc–area hikes are dry, cool, and clear. Wildflowers have shifted to autumn yellows and reds.
Returning UN agenda and World Trade Organisation activity make Geneva energetic, but weekend tourism remains comfortable.
Some autumn arts and architectural light installations begin in late September, previewing winter cultural programming.

reasons_to_reconsider.details: From mid-month, UN events and watch industry traffic push weekday hotel rates near year-high, particularly in Pâquis and around Place des Nations.
Mornings drop to 10–12°C; lake-front evenings need a light jacket. Lake swimming still possible but cooler.
Musiques en été and OpenAir Cinema have ended; outdoor concerts limited to occasional festival days.
Some Atlantic fronts arrive late month, ending the dry spell. Pack for variable weather.
Post-summer business travel and conference visitors snap up the best fine-dining tables; book ahead.
Boat schedules contract from late September onwards as the autumn timetable takes over.

weather.precipitation: Mostly dry, with stable high pressure. Lake at 21°C.
weather.general_tips: Light layers; light evening jacket. Sunglasses and sunscreen still essential.

tourism_level.crowds: Moderate. Weekend leisure travel strong; weekdays driven by business and diplomatic visitors.
tourism_level.pricing: Climbing on weekdays; reasonable on weekends.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Glorious early autumn — the Geneva rentrée energy combined with summer-soft evenings.

events_holidays.name: Geneva Wine Harvest Programming
events_holidays.description: Vineyards across Mandement and Satigny host harvest events, sometimes including open-pickings and tasting weekends.
events_holidays.notes: Reserve where required; many events run Friday evening through Sunday.

events_holidays.name: OSR Season Launch
events_holidays.description: Orchestre de la Suisse Romande opens its concert season at Victoria Hall.

events_holidays.name: Geneva International Film Festival Tous Écrans (variable)
events_holidays.description: International film and series festival across cinemas in Geneva.

unique_experiences.description: Walk between family vineyards harvesting and pressing, with tastings of the previous year's vintage and a chance to see this year's grapes coming in.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Train from Cornavin to Satigny. Some estates require reservation.

unique_experiences.description: Climb the tower for the lake-and-Mont-Blanc panorama, then descend to explore the remarkable 4th-century archaeological site beneath the cathedral.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Combined ticket. Allow 2 hours.

unique_experiences.where: Col de la Faucille, Le Reculet, La Dôle
unique_experiences.description: Bus and cable car into the Jura ridge above Geneva for one of the best Mont Blanc panoramas in Europe.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Allow full day. Bring proper hiking shoes and layers.

unique_experiences.description: Long calm sunsets and Mont Blanc reflections — late summer's perfect lakefront moment.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Free walking; nearby buvettes for drinks.

weather.precipitation: First Atlantic fronts arrive. Generally still pleasant with some rainy stretches.
weather.general_tips: Pack a light rain layer. Cooler nights — long-sleeve essentials.

tourism_level.crowds: Moderate to busy on weekdays driven by business travel; quieter weekends.
tourism_level.pricing: Weekday hotels at year-high or near it; weekends softer.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Energetic, professional Geneva — packed cafés in the morning, lake walks at lunch, concert hall and opera in the evening.

events_holidays.name: Jeûne Genevois (Geneva Fast)
events_holidays.date: Thursday after first Sunday of September
events_holidays.description: Geneva-specific public holiday with widespread closures. Traditional plum tart (tarte aux pruneaux) eaten at home.
events_holidays.notes: Most shops closed; museums open. Plan a quiet day or day-trip.

events_holidays.name: Watches & Wonders Adjacent Events
events_holidays.description: Industry brand pop-ups and showcases continue post-spring fair.
events_holidays.notes: Check Rue du Rhône boutique schedules.

events_holidays.name: Marathon de Genève (variable)
events_holidays.description: Geneva's main marathon — full and half — winds through the city and lakefront.
events_holidays.notes: Spectator-friendly. Tram diversions on race day.

unique_experiences.where: Patisseries across the city (Auer, Aux Trois Brins)
unique_experiences.description: The Jeûne Genevois tradition: a buttery short-pastry plum tart eaten throughout Geneva on the holiday weekend.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Order in advance for whole tarts.

unique_experiences.description: Take the Geneva → Brig → Bellinzona train (4 hours one way) for a long-day excursion to Italian-speaking Switzerland's UNESCO castles.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Long but stunning. Best on a Saturday with clear weather.

unique_experiences.where: Patek Philippe Museum + Rue du Rhône
unique_experiences.description: Combine the museum visit with a curated walk of major Genevan haute-horlogerie boutiques.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Reserve museum visits in advance.

unique_experiences.where: Le Verre à Monique (Carouge), La Cave de la Vigneronne (Eaux-Vives)
unique_experiences.description: Compare Genevan Gamay, Gamaret, and Pinot Noir from the just-pressing 2024 (when available) and recent vintages.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Reserve weekends; cheese boards pair well.

reasons_to_visit.reason: Autumn Colour Along the Lake & in the Jura
reasons_to_visit.details: Lakefront plane trees, parks, and the Jura forests light up in golds and reds — particularly stunning on Salève cable-car rides and Lavaux vineyard walks.

reasons_to_visit.reason: Nuit des Bains (Quartier des Bains Open Night)
reasons_to_visit.details: Three times a year (typically May, September, and November) but October programmes still energise Geneva's contemporary art quarter with openings, performances, and crowded after-parties.
October is peak hunting season — venison, wild boar, chamois, and pheasant fill menus at traditional bistros and Michelin-starred restaurants alike.
Cool crisp days reward Old Town walking and museum visits, with terraces still open on sunny afternoons.
Outside major conferences, hotel rates ease meaningfully from September highs — a good urban-luxury value window.
Opera, orchestra, dance, and museum programmes are fully back. Grand Théâtre, BFM, and Victoria Hall all run flagship productions.

reasons_to_reconsider.details: Geneva basin inversions become common mid-October, sometimes shrouding the city for days while sunshine persists above 700m.
Sunset moves earlier through the month, with daylight saving ending late October cutting the evening light meaningfully.
Genève-Plage and Plage des Eaux-Vives close. Bains des Pâquis swimming for the very hardy.
Snow can arrive at 1,500m by month-end; higher trails close or require winter equipment.
Chasse season fills traditional restaurants quickly; book a week or more in advance for weekend dinners.
Time change on the last weekend creates transient train and meeting disruption.

weather.precipitation: Variable: warm sunny afternoons alongside cooler rainy fronts. Some lingering bise.
weather.general_tips: Layered clothing; light waterproof. Sunglasses still useful in clear afternoons.

tourism_level.crowds: Moderate; conferences and watch industry keep weekday hotels busy.
tourism_level.pricing: Mid-range, climbing with major business events.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Energetic urban autumn — café season, terraces still in use, lakefront runners and walkers.

events_holidays.name: Marathon de Genève (variable)
events_holidays.description: City marathon and half-marathon — major spectator-friendly event.
events_holidays.notes: Tram detours on race day; check timing if needing the lakefront.

events_holidays.name: BFM Opera & Concert Premieres
events_holidays.description: Major opera and concert premieres at Bâtiment des Forces Motrices.
events_holidays.notes: Book in advance for opening nights.

events_holidays.name: Watch Industry Events
events_holidays.description: Industry showcases drive weekday hotel demand.
events_holidays.notes: Lakefront and Pâquis hotels firm up early.

unique_experiences.where: Café du Soleil, Brasserie Lipp, Les Armures (Old Town)
unique_experiences.description: Traditional Geneva game season — venison medallions with red cabbage and spätzli, wild boar terrines, and pheasant ragouts on every classic menu.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Reserve a week ahead for weekends. Pair with local Gamaret.

unique_experiences.description: Golden-orange beech and oak forests, big views, and crisp air. Lunch at La Corraterie or pack a picnic.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Bring passport. Bus 8 to terminus. Last cable car down earlier in autumn — check.

unique_experiences.where: Espace Saint-Pierre & Musée International de la Réforme
unique_experiences.description: Combine cathedral, Site Archéologique, and the Reformation Museum at Maison Mallet for a deep dive into Geneva's Calvinist history.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Allow 3 hours combined. Closed Mondays.

unique_experiences.description: Student-and-creatives-energised neighbourhood with strong wine bars, natural-wine spots, and great cheese boards.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Tram 12 or 15. Many bars run all-night Thursdays.

weather.precipitation: Cooler. Frequent fog. Snowline drops in surrounding Alps; first frosts possible in the basin.
weather.general_tips: Bring warmer layers, waterproof shell, and grippy shoes for damp cobblestones.

tourism_level.crowds: Quieter on weekends; conferences still drive weekdays.
tourism_level.pricing: Soft on weekends; weekday rates conference-dependent.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Late autumn — fondue starting to reappear, fog rolling off the lake, Old Town lit warmly at dusk.

events_holidays.name: Daylight Saving Ends
events_holidays.description: Clocks fall back; sunsets jump to around 17:30.
events_holidays.notes: Evenings shorten markedly; train and meeting transitions need attention.

events_holidays.name: Nuit des Bains (when in October)
events_holidays.description: Quartier des Bains galleries open late with performances and after-parties.

events_holidays.name: Halloween in Bars & Bistros
events_holidays.description: Carouge bars and Pâquis lounges run themed nights — moderate Halloween presence.

unique_experiences.description: Geneva's basin fog makes the perfect day to take three top museums back-to-back.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Closed Mondays. Combine with a fondue dinner.

unique_experiences.description: The terraced vineyards turn a copper-gold over the lake — one of Switzerland's signature autumn walks.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Trains every 30 min. Allow 3–4 hours walking plus tasting stops.

unique_experiences.description: Contemporary art galleries open late, with performances, DJs, and crowded after-parties.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Free entry. Map at any participating gallery.

unique_experiences.description: Sauna and hammam season is fully back. Sweat with a lake view, then plunge into the cool lake.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Bring sandals, towel, and swimwear.

reasons_to_visit.details: One of Europe's largest urban running events takes over the Old Town on the first weekend of December but November weekends are full of preparation runs, costume parades, and family festival energy.
The city's light-art festival illuminates monuments, parks, and public buildings with site-specific installations through late November and December — a magical reason to visit in low light.
Game menus continue at their richest. November is peak season for partridge, hare, and the late-autumn root vegetables.
Geneva's main Christmas market at Jardin Anglais opens in mid-to-late November, with chalets, vin chaud, raclette, and crafts running through December.
Between conferences and pre-Christmas tourism, mid-November is one of the year's best urban-luxury value windows.
Glacier resorts like Zermatt and Saas-Fee open early; cross-country trails appear in the Jura. Day trips begin to work for early-season turns.

reasons_to_reconsider.details: Geneva basin frequently sits under cold low cloud for days, while the Jura and Salève bask in sunshine above the inversion layer.
Sunset by 17:00 limits sightseeing windows. Lighting at the Lux Festival becomes one of the principal evening draws.
Lakefront kiosks shut; Bains des Pâquis switches firmly to winter sauna mode.
Cold northeasterly winds make outdoor walking feel rawer than the thermometer suggests.
From mid-November, the city centre and Rues Basses fill with Christmas shoppers — particularly weekends.
CGN runs only minimal winter service.

weather.precipitation: Cool and often grey. Rain and fog dominate; some clear bise days.
weather.general_tips: Pack waterproof shell, warm layers, and grippy shoes for damp cobblestones.

tourism_level.crowds: Light. Conferences drive some weekday demand.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Quietly working late autumn. Fondue, fog, and warm light from Old Town windows.

events_holidays.name: All Saints' Day (Toussaint)
events_holidays.description: Catholic observance — not a Geneva public holiday but observed in border France with full closures.
events_holidays.notes: Cross-border day trips affected. No impact within the city.

events_holidays.name: Lux Festival Opens (variable)
events_holidays.description: Light installations across the city — monuments, parks, public buildings.
events_holidays.notes: Free; programme released two weeks ahead. Plan an evening walking route.

events_holidays.name: Chasse Season Continues
events_holidays.notes: Book a week ahead for weekends.

unique_experiences.description: Indoor day with the world's premier watch museum — three hours pass effortlessly.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Closed Sun/Mon.

unique_experiences.description: When Geneva sits under fog, the Salève plateau often basks in winter sunshine.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Bring passport, sunglasses, warm layers.

unique_experiences.where: Les Armures, Café du Bourg-de-Four, Café Papon
unique_experiences.description: Combine a walk past the cathedral and Treille promenade with a cosy fondue in a 17th-century cave.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Reserve weekends.

unique_experiences.description: Perfect indoor day for the most thought-provoking museum in Geneva.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Closed Mondays. Allow 2 hours.

weather.precipitation: Cold, frequent fog and overcast. Snow possible at city level late month; reliable above 1,000m.
weather.general_tips: Full winter gear. Plan around indoor and illuminated outdoor experiences.

tourism_level.crowds: Christmas market and pre-Escalade build-up bring weekend energy to the Old Town.
tourism_level.pricing: Climbing slightly as Christmas tourism begins.
tourism_level.overall_atmosphere: Pre-festive. Decorations going up; mulled wine appearing on terraces; Lux Festival lighting the city after dark.

events_holidays.name: Geneva Christmas Markets Open
events_holidays.description: Main market at Jardin Anglais; smaller markets at Fusterie and Noël au Quai.
events_holidays.notes: Bring cash and warm layers. Weekend evenings busiest.

events_holidays.name: Lux Festival in Full Swing
events_holidays.description: Site-specific light installations across the city — Old Town, Plainpalais, Pâquis.
events_holidays.notes: Free. Plan a self-guided evening walking route.

events_holidays.name: Course de l'Escalade Preparation
events_holidays.description: Training runs and costume rehearsals visible across the Old Town in the lead-up to the December race.
events_holidays.notes: Festive atmosphere in the cobbled streets.

unique_experiences.description: Geneva's main Christmas market: chalets, vin chaud, raclette, crafts, and a Ferris wheel with lake views.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Cash useful. Evening fairy lights are magical.

unique_experiences.where: Old Town to Pâquis (route varies year to year)
unique_experiences.description: Walk a self-curated route through 10–15 light installations: projection-mapped façades, illuminated trees, interactive sculptures.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Free. Bring warm layers; allow 2 hours.

unique_experiences.activity: Bains des Pâquis Winter Sauna + Soup
unique_experiences.description: Winter sauna with a lake view, then a bowl of the famous soup at the buvette.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Bring towel, swimwear, sandals. Sauna schedule extends into the evening.

unique_experiences.description: Independent watchmakers, jewellers, milliners, and chocolatiers prepare festive collections.
unique_experiences.practical_tips: Tram 12. Many ateliers Wed–Sat only.

overview_paragraph: Geneva is at its best in late spring (May–June) and early autumn (September), when the lake warms, Mont Blanc stays clear, and café terraces along the quais run late. July and August bring fireworks, Musiques en été concerts, and lake swimming—balanced against peak hotel rates and August 1 closures. Winter (December–February) is fondue, Escalade festival, and ski-resort access from Cornavin; March eases the city back into the open with longer light and softer prices.

categories.title: Must Do
categories.description: Essential Geneva experiences—iconic, memorable, and easy to recommend.

items.description: Walk the pier to the foot of Geneva's 140-metre fountain, with Mont Blanc behind on clear days.
items.optimal_time: Sunny afternoons, year-round (March-October at full height)

items.description: Climb the cathedral tower for the lake-and-Mont-Blanc panorama, then descend to the 4th-century archaeological site below.

items.activity: Cross the harbour by Mouettes water-bus
items.description: Hop the yellow Mouettes Genevoises between Pâquis, Eaux-Vives, and Molard—every 10 minutes and covered by the Geneva Transport Card.

items.activity: Reformation Wall at Parc des Bastions
items.description: Massive carved monument to Calvin, Knox, and the Reformers—paired with the giant outdoor chess boards and chestnut-shaded paths of Parc des Bastions.

categories.title: Best in Summer
categories.description: Prime months: June-August. Lake swimming, late terraces, and Alpine day-trips.

items.description: The city's beloved jetty bath—swim, sunbathe, sauna, and grab a fondue at the on-site buvette.
Free classical, jazz, and world-music concerts in courtyards across the city.
Geneva's biggest festival closes with a massive lakefront fireworks display in August.

items.activity: Salève cable car to Mont Blanc panorama
items.description: Five-minute cable car ascent into France for the iconic Geneva-and-Mont-Blanc panorama.

categories.title: Best in Winter
categories.description: Fondue, festivals, and ski-resort access on Geneva's doorstep.

items.description: Geneva's biggest winter tradition—re-enactment of the 1602 victory over Savoy, with costumed parades, bonfires, and chocolate marmite-smashing.
Lakefront chalets, glüwein, raclette, and lights all along Quai du Mont-Blanc.

items.activity: Fondue at Les Armures or Café du Soleil
items.description: Two of the city's most beloved fondue institutions—wood-panelled, candlelit, and reliably excellent.

items.activity: Ski day in Chamonix or Verbier
items.description: Direct shuttle from Geneva Airport (60-90 min) puts you at Mont Blanc's lifts before lunch.

categories.title: Rainy Day Favorites
categories.description: Cozy, mostly indoor picks for fog or Atlantic-front days.

items.description: The world's pre-eminent horology museum—centuries of watchmaking, automata, and miniature enamel art.
Geneva's largest museum—painting, antiquities, applied arts. Free permanent collection.
Family-run since 1820—legendary thick hot chocolate and hand-made chocolates in a wood-panelled salon.

items.activity: International Red Cross & Red Crescent Museum
items.description: Immersive, deeply moving museum on humanitarian action, near the UN.

categories.title: Local Experiences
categories.description: Everyday Geneva: markets, vineyards, lakefront walks, and Carouge bistros.

items.description: Seasonal produce, cheese, charcuterie, flowers, and a strong neighbourhood atmosphere.
Train 15 min from Cornavin to Geneva's wine country—family vineyards pouring Chasselas, Gamay, and Gamaret with views to the Jura.
Geneva's biggest flea market—antiques, vinyl, vintage clothes, and a strong food-truck scene Wednesdays and Saturdays.
Le Verre à Monique and the lanes around Place du Marché—Genevan whites, regional cheese boards, and the city's most relaxed nightlife.
